role: ArchSpine is a semantic mirror system that automatically analyzes, validates, and synchronizes architectural metadata across software projects, enabling consistent governance and documentation for both human developers and AI agents.
responsibility: The system is composed of several coordinated layers: a CLI layer (src/cli) handles user commands (check, fix, sync, init) and bootstrapping; a service layer (src/services) orchestrates end-to-end pipelines; an engine layer (src/engines) provides file scanning, rule enforcement, and fix generation; a core aggregation layer (src/core) collects and validates data from multiple sources into canonical L2 state; an infrastructure layer (src/infra) provides configuration, credential storage, SQLite persistence, LLM provider interaction, MCP server exposure, prompt construction, and manifest management; an AST layer (src/ast) parses source code into structured symbols; a task pipeline (src/tasks) implements scanning, extraction, summarization, validation, fixing, backfilling, indexing, and view derivation stages; and a type system (src/types) defines data contracts. The example project (examples/demo-project) illustrates the .spine directory structure and rules. Tests (tests/) validate all components from unit to end-to-end. CI/CD workflows (.github/workflows) automate linting, building, testing, documentation verification, and release checks.
